bibliographic_data.title: A Hybrid Optimization and Deep Learning Algorithm for Cyber-resilient DER Control
bibliographic_data.abstract: With the proliferation of distributed energy resources (DERs) in the distribution grid, it is a challenge to effectively control a large number of DERs resilient to communication and security disruptions. To this end, a hybrid feedback-based optimization algorithm along with deep learning forecasting technique is proposed to specifically address the cyber-related issues. The online decentralized feedback-based DER optimization control requires timely, accurate voltage measurement from the grid. However, in practice such information may not be received by the control center or even be corrupted. Therefore, the long short-term memory (LSTM) deep learning algorithm is employed to forecast delayed/missed/attacked messages with high accuracy.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a hybrid feedback-based optimization and deep learning algorithm for cyber-resilient DER control, specifically addressing the issues of delayed or lost voltage measurements.
core_content.key_methodology: A long short-term memory (LSTM) deep learning algorithm is employed to forecast delayed/missed/attacked messages with high accuracy.
outcomes: The LSTM-forecasted lost voltage can effectively improve the performance of the DER control algorithm in practical cyber-physical architecture.
The LSTM forecasting strategy outperforms other strategies using previous message and skipping dual parameter update.
core_content.full_text_markdown: In this paper, a hybrid feedback-based optimization and deep learning algorithm is proposed for cyber-resilient distributed energy resource (DER) control. The challenge of handling delayed or lost voltage measurements in the grid is addressed by employing an LSTM deep learning algorithm to forecast these messages accurately. This approach aims to improve the performance of DER control algorithms in practical cyber-physical architectures, ensuring reliable and resilient operation of the integrated grid.
